name: infrastructure-management
description: Manage network hosts and devices across all UniFi sites. Monitor host status, device configuration, and infrastructure health for comprehensive inventory management.
capabilities: List all hosts across all UniFi sites
Get detailed device configuration and status
Monitor host and device health status
author: DataKnifeAI
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation files (SKILL.md, skill-report.json) with no executable code, network calls, or file system access. All 31 static findings are false positives triggered by documentation formatting and metadata fields.
valueStatement: Keep track of all network hosts and devices across your UniFi infrastructure. Get comprehensive visibility into device status, configuration, and health across multiple sites.
limitations: Requires UniFi controller with API access

useCases.title: Infrastructure Inventory
useCases.description: Maintain complete visibility of all network devices and hosts across multiple sites for accurate inventory tracking.

useCases.title: Multi-Site Monitoring
useCases.description: Monitor device health and connectivity across distributed UniFi deployments from a centralized view.

useCases.title: Deployment Planning
useCases.description: Analyze resource distribution and plan capacity expansion based on current infrastructure utilization.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Inventory Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me all hosts and devices across my UniFi infrastructure

promptTemplates.title: Site-Specific Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: List all devices and hosts in the New York site with their current status

promptTemplates.title: Device Troubleshooting
promptTemplates.prompt: Get detailed information about device AP-123 including configuration and connected hosts

promptTemplates.title: Infrastructure Planning
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze my infrastructure inventory by site and show resource distribution for expansion planning

bestPractices: Use consistent device naming conventions across all sites
Document device purposes and physical locations in descriptions
Regularly review device configurations for standardization
antiPatterns: Avoid generic device names that do not indicate location or purpose
Do not ignore offline devices - investigate connectivity issues promptly
Never share controller credentials or API keys in chat

faq.question: Which UniFi controller versions are supported?
faq.answer: Works with UniFi Network Controller 6.0+ and UniFi OS Console devices. Requires API access enabled.

faq.question: Can I modify device configurations through this skill?
faq.answer: This skill provides read-only access for monitoring and inventory. Use UniFi controller for configuration changes.

faq.question: How does the skill connect to my UniFi infrastructure?
faq.answer: Connects via UniFi controller API using your controller URL and API credentials configured in the MCP server.

faq.question: Is my infrastructure data secure?
faq.answer: All data remains in your local MCP server. No information is transmitted to external services or stored permanently.

faq.question: What if devices show as offline?
faq.answer: Check physical connections, power status, and network connectivity. Verify device adoption status in UniFi controller.

faq.question: How is this different from the UniFi controller interface?
faq.answer: Provides conversational access to infrastructure data and can combine information from multiple sites in unified views.
